content_en:
When Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah # read the letter he said sorrowfully that he knew what was the urgent and important work that ‘Umar Farooq 8 had for me. He wanted to keep alive a man who was not born to live in this world forever. Then he wrote a letter to the Caliph ‘Umar Farooq -%. After greeting him he said he knew what work he had for me. He was in the Muslim army and at that time the Muslims were involved in severe problems; he could not leave them alone at that critical phase, nor could he separate himself from them, unless it was Allah Almighty’s Will to decide about their fate and his. He appealed to the Caliph to consider him helpless to obey his commands in this matter and requested him to give him permission to stay with the Mujahideen of Islam.

When ‘Umar Farooq # received this letter and read it his eyes brimmed with tears. The Companions sitting around him were surprised to see this and asked him if here was some tragic news, and if the Commander of the Muslim forces, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4& had died. He answered:

“No, but death is very near him now.”

And his guess turned out to be correct. A few days later Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 3 was struck by the deadly plague.

In his last days he made the following dying statement to his armed forces:

“Today I am giving you some advice; if you accept you it will find peace and security to be your companions:

Establish Salét, be regular in your prayers.

Fast during the month of Ramadan.

Give sacrifices and be charitable often,

Encourage and instruct each other to do good actions. Wish your rulers well and be loyal to them.

Never deceive your rulers.

Be careful that you perform your duties and are not lost in worldly pursuits.

@ Listen carefully to what I have to say next. Even if a man gets to live to be a thousand years old, one day he will be in the same position in which you see me today. No one can escape death.

My greetings of peace to all of you; may there be countless Blessings of Allah the Merciful on you all.”

Then he turned to Mu’adh bin Jabl 8 and said: “Lead the men well in prayers. Allah Hafiz.”

And with these words on his lips he passed away. “To Allah we belong and to Him we return.”

Mu’adh bin Jabl 4 stood up and told the people with quivering lips and a trembling voice:

“Brothers in Islam! You are sunk in grief today for a man who was a more good hearted than any of us. He had a pure heart free from malice or envy; and he was a man who loved the Hereafter and was the well-wisher of each one of us. He was a man who was given the title of ‘Amin-ul-Ummah’ and during his very lifetime on this earth he was given the glad tidings that he would go to Paradise by no less a person than the Prophet 3 himself. He was a very lucky man indeed! Let us all pray that his position in Paradise ever exalted. May Allah 3% shower his Blessings on Him.”

Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas Messenger of Allah # said:

“Sa‘d! shoot your arrows, May my mother and

father be sacrificed for you.”

Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas

A tall tanned sturdily built youth with broad shoulders and curly hair he was a picture of devotion to Allah Almighty. He led a austere and pure life, kept awake at nights in worship, and was courageous and yet forbearing. A God-fearing man he was generous, humble and selfsacrificing. This fortunate Companion had received the glad tidings that he would go to Paradise, from the lips of the Prophet 38 himself. He had also prayed to Allah Almighty that the supplications of Sa’d bin Abi Wagas should be answered and that his arrow should always find its mark. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas had been a member of the group of Companion who had pledged [loyalty to Allah 3 and His Prophet 3 at what came to be known as_ the Bai‘at-Rizwan. These Companions are mentioned in the Noble Quran, and Sa‘d bin Abi Wagas 3% was one of them. He was one of those brave soldiers who took part in the Battle of Uhud and were known for their fearlessness. In this battle this experienced archer had rained his arrows on the enemy and the Prophet 3% had been so pleased with him that he said to him:

“Sa'd shoot your arrows; May my mother and father be

Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas .# became a Muslim at the age of seventeen. He was educated and trained in the Dar Argam' and was with the Prophet 38

' Argaam was the name of a Companion. The newly converted Muslims used o meet secretly at his house to study the teachings of Islam. They were forced to meet in secret because of their persecution by the Quraish. (Translator’s note}

at the Shi‘ab Abi Talib,' bearing with patience and fortitude the difficulties and hardship of the siege. The whole of his life was spent displaying his prowess and achieving amazing exploits in the battlefield. Every moment of his life deserves study by the Muslims of today and especially the youth in the military.

In the famous place called Jasr in Iraq he opposed the Iraqi army. They came on to the battlefield looking very warlike and arrogantly clapping their breasts. There was very heavy fighting and the attack of the enemy was so very forceful and severe that four thousand Mujahideen were martyred ina single day. The Caliph, ‘Umar Farooq - was very sad to hear this tragic news, and decided to go to Iraq and lead the Muslim army himself. He ordered that ‘Ali # be putin charge of affairs at the capital, Al-Madinah, and with some Mujahideen reinforcements set out for Iraq. When ‘Abdur-Rahman_ bin ‘Auf 4 came to know that ‘Umar Farooq was on his way to Iraq, he was very upset and set out to stop him. He caught up with him just a short distance from Al-Madinah and persuaded him not to leave the capital, as the situation was critical. He tried to convince him that his presence was essential in the capital, as the Muslims were facing a difficult and delicate situation. The Muslim Ummali needed his services very badly, and there were plenty of experienced strategists, and courageous lions, who could be sent to crush the kuffar. He suggested it would be better to entrust the military leadership to an experienced and fearless General. These arguments convinced ‘Umar Farooq + and he summoned ‘Ali 4 and some other prominent Companion to a meeting; they all agreed that ‘Umar Farooq -# should return to the capital, and an experienced commander be chosen to lead the Muslim forces in Iraq. Giving in to the collective opinion of the Companions, ‘Umar Farooq # returned to Al-Madinah. Once there he called another meeting of the Companion to advise him on the choice of a General. He asked them to suggest he name of a heroic general who

* The Muslims (men, women and children) were besieged in the house of Abi Talib in Makkah. It was a very difficult time and the siege lasted three years.

Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas -, the great General, was the cousin brother of the Prophet's mother, Aminah. Once when he came with some of his companions into the presence of the Prophet 38, he very proudly told those present that he was his uncle and asked if anybody had a better uncle, they should introduce him.

After pledging allegiance to Islam in the flower of his youth and receiving his education and training in Dar-ul-Arqam, he had the privilege of being among the first distinguished Companion to accept Islam. Among those to be influenced by the teachings of Abu Bakr Siddiq > were ‘Uthman bin ‘Affan, Zubair bin ‘Awam, ‘AbdurRahman bin ‘Auf, Talhah bin ‘Abdullah and Sa‘d bin Abi Wags 4 2, -+—. These first Companion, in order to escape the persecution of the Quraish used to hide in the deserted areas near Makkah to pray. On one occasion he was in a secret hiding place with some of his companions absorbed in meditation and prayer, when some mischievous and rowdy elements spotted them. They started to scornfully jeer at them. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas -# picked up the bone of a camel lying nearby and threw it forcefully like an arrow. It hit one of the boys on his forehead and blood spurted out. Panic-stricken they all fled, stumbling and tumbling against each other. This was the first courageous step taken by Sa‘d bin Abi Waqs - in support of Islam to cow down its enemies, and it was a very effective step.

Sa’d bin Abi Waqas # was a man who possessed many great qualities, but there are two special traits of his character which make

him stand out among the others. The first one is that when the Prophet 3 saw his marksmanship in the battle of Uhud, he was so impressed and so happy that he wished him to continue his good work and said even his parents could be sacrificed for him. It is certainly a great honour which distinguishes him from others. Another good quality was that he was blessed with the ability to pray to Allah 38 and have his supplications answered. The Prophet 3% had prayed to Allah 3% that He should grant acceptance to the entreaties of Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 3. After this whenever Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas prayed to Allah Almighty, his prayers and supplications would be answered. All other Companion used to value him highly.
content_id:
Ketika Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah membaca surat tersebut, ia berkata dengan sedih bahwa ia mengetahui pekerjaan mendesak dan penting yang 'Umar Farooq maksudkan untuknya. Beliau ingin menjaga kehidupan seseorang yang tidak dilahirkan untuk hidup selamanya di dunia ini. Kemudian ia menulis surat kepada Khalifah 'Umar Farooq. Setelah menyampaikan salam, ia menyatakan bahwa ia mengetahui pekerjaan yang dimaksudkan untuknya. Ia sedang berada di tengah pasukan Muslim dan pada saat itu kaum Muslim sedang menghadapi masalah yang sangat berat; ia tidak bisa meninggalkan mereka sendirian di fase kritis tersebut, dan tidak pula bisa memisahkan diri dari mereka, kecuali jika Allah Yang Mahakuasa berkehendak memutuskan nasib mereka dan nasibnya. Ia memohon kepada Khalifah agar memaklumi ketidakmampuannya mematuhi perintah beliau dalam masalah ini dan meminta izin untuk tetap bersama para mujahidin Islam.

Ketika 'Umar Farooq menerima surat ini dan membacanya, matanya berlinang air mata. Para sahabat yang duduk di sekelilingnya terkejut melihat hal itu dan bertanya apakah ada berita tragis, dan apakah Panglima pasukan Muslim, Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah, telah wafat. Beliau menjawab:

"Belum, tetapi kematian sudah sangat dekat dengannya sekarang."

Dan dugaannya ternyata benar. Beberapa hari kemudian Abu 'Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah tertimpa wabah yang mematikan.

Di hari-hari terakhirnya, beliau menyampaikan wasiat berikut kepada pasukannya:

"Hari ini aku memberikan beberapa nasihat kepada kalian; jika kalian menerimanya, kalian akan mendapati kedamaian dan keselamatan menjadi teman kalian:

Tegakkanlah salah, jadilah teratur dalam salah kalian.

Berpuasalah di bulan Ramadan.

Berkurbanlah dan bersedekahlah dengan sering.

Saling mendorong dan menasihati untuk melakukan amal kebaikan. Doakanlah kebaikan bagi pemimpin kalian dan setialah kepada mereka.

Jangan pernah menipu pemimpin kalian.

Berhati-hatilah agar kalian menunaikan kewajiban kalian dan tidak tenggelam dalam urusan duniawi.

Dengarkanlah baik-baik apa yang akan kukatakan selanjutnya. Sekalipun seseorang hidup hingga seribu tahun, suatu hari ia akan berada dalam posisi yang sama seperti yang kalian lihat padaku hari ini. Tidak ada seorang pun yang dapat luput dari kematian.

Salamku untuk kalian semua; semoga rahmat Allah Yang Maha Pengasih tercurah tanpa batas atas kalian semua."

Kemudian beliau menoleh kepada Mu'adh bin Jabal dan berkata: "Pimpinlah orang-orang dalam salah dengan baik. Allah Hafiz."

Dan dengan kata-kata itulah beliau berpulang ke rahmatullah. "Sesungguhnya kita milik Allah dan kepada-Nya kita kembali."

Mu'adh bin Jabal berdiri dan berkata kepada orang-orang dengan bibir bergetar dan suara yang gemetar:

"Saudara-saudara seIslam! Kalian tenggelam dalam duka hari ini atas kepergian seorang pria yang hatinya lebih baik dari siapa pun di antara kita. Beliau memiliki hati yang bersih, bebas dari kedengkian dan iri hati; dan beliau adalah seorang yang mencintai akhirat serta pendoa kebaikan bagi setiap kita. Beliau adalah seorang yang diberi gelar 'Amin-ul-Ummah' dan semasa hidupnya di dunia ini beliau telah diberi kabar gembira bahwa beliau akan masuk surga oleh tidak lain Nabi sendiri. Beliau sungguh seorang yang sangat beruntung! Marilah kita semua berdoa agar kedudukannya di surga senantiasa ditinggikan. Semoga Allah melimpahkan rahmat-Nya kepadanya."

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas, Rasulullah bersabda:

"Sa'd! Lepaskanlah anak panahmu, Semoga ayah dan

ibuku menjadi tebusanmu."

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas

Seorang pemuda bertubuh tinggi, berkulit kecokelatan, berbadan tegap dengan bahu yang lebar dan rambut ikal — beliau adalah gambaran ketaatan kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa. Beliau menjalani kehidupan yang sederhana dan bersih, terjaga di malam hari untuk beribadah, pemberani namun tetap penyabar. Seorang yang bertaqwa, beliau dermawan, rendah hati, dan rela berkorban. Sahabat yang beruntung ini telah menerima kabar gembira bahwa beliau akan masuk surga, langsung dari lisan Nabi sendiri. Beliau juga pernah berdoa kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa agar doa-doa Sa'd bin Abi Waqas dikabulkan dan agar anak panahnya selalu mengenai sasaran. Sa'd bin Abi Waqas adalah anggota dari kelompok sahabat yang telah berjanji setia kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya dalam peristiwa yang dikenal sebagai Bai'at-Ridhwan. Para sahabat ini disebutkan dalam Al-Quran yang mulia, dan Sa'd bin Abi Waqas adalah salah satu dari mereka. Beliau termasuk prajurit pemberani yang ikut serta dalam Perang Uhud dan dikenal karena keberanian mereka yang tanpa rasa takut. Dalam pertempuran ini, pemanah berpengalaman ini menghujani musuh dengan anak panahnya dan Nabi begitu senang dengannya sehingga beliau bersabda kepadanya:

"Sa'd, lepaskanlah anak panahmu; Semoga ayah dan ibuku menjadi tebusanmu."

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas masuk Islam pada usia tujuh belas tahun. Beliau dididik dan dilatih di Dar Al-Arqam dan bersama Nabi

Al-Arqam adalah nama seorang sahabat. Kaum Muslim yang baru masuk Islam biasa berkumpul secara diam-diam di rumahnya untuk mempelajari ajaran Islam. Mereka terpaksa bertemu secara rahasia karena penganiayaan oleh kaum Quraisy. (Catatan penerjemah)

di Shi'ab Abi Thalib, menanggung dengan sabar dan tabah segala kesulitan dan penderitaan selama pengepungan. Seluruh hidupnya dihabiskan untuk menunjukkan keberanian dan meraih pencapaian luar biasa di medan perang. Setiap momen hidupnya layak dipelajari oleh kaum Muslim masa kini, terutama para pemuda di bidang militer.

Di tempat terkenal bernama Jasr di Irak, beliau menghadapi pasukan Irak. Mereka datang ke medan perang dengan penampilan sangat garang dan dengan sombong memukul-mukul dada mereka. Pertempuran berlangsung sangat sengit dan serangan musuh begitu dahsyat dan ganas sehingga empat ribu mujahidin gugur sebagai syuhada dalam satu hari. Khalifah 'Umar Farooq sangat sedih mendengar berita tragis ini, dan memutuskan untuk pergi ke Irak dan memimpin sendiri pasukan Muslim. Beliau memerintahkan agar 'Ali ditempatkan sebagai penanggung jawab urusan di ibu kota, Al-Madinah, dan dengan beberapa pasukan bantuan mujahidin berangkat menuju Irak. Ketika 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf mengetahui bahwa 'Umar Farooq sedang dalam perjalanan ke Irak, ia sangat khawatir dan berangkat untuk menghentikannya. Ia berhasil menyusulnya tidak jauh dari Al-Madinah dan membujuknya agar tidak meninggalkan ibu kota, karena situasinya sangat genting. Ia berusaha meyakinkan bahwa kehadiran beliau sangat diperlukan di ibu kota, karena kaum Muslim sedang menghadapi situasi yang sulit dan rumit. Ummah Muslim sangat membutuhkan pengabdian beliau, dan masih banyak ahli strategi yang berpengalaman serta singa-singa pemberani yang bisa dikirim untuk menghancurkan kaum kuffar. Ia menyarankan agar lebih baik menyerahkan kepemimpinan militer kepada seorang jenderal yang berpengalaman dan tanpa rasa takut. Argumen-argumen ini meyakinkan 'Umar Farooq dan beliau memanggil 'Ali serta beberapa sahabat terkemuka lainnya untuk bermusyawarah; mereka semua sepakat bahwa 'Umar Farooq harus kembali ke ibu kota, dan seorang panglima berpengalaman dipilih untuk memimpin pasukan Muslim di Irak. Mengalah pada pendapat bersama para sahabat, 'Umar Farooq kembali ke Al-Madinah. Sesampainya di sana, beliau mengadakan pertemuan lagi dengan para sahabat untuk meminta saran tentang pemilihan seorang jenderal. Beliau meminta mereka menyebutkan nama seorang jenderal yang heroik yang

* Kaum Muslim (laki-laki, perempuan, dan anak-anak) dikepung di rumah Abu Thalib di Makkah. Itu adalah masa yang sangat sulit dan pengepungan itu berlangsung selama tiga tahun.

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas, jenderal agung itu, adalah saudara sepupu dari ibu Nabi, Aminah. Suatu ketika ketika beliau datang bersama beberapa sahabatnya menghadap Nabi, beliau dengan sangat bangga berkata kepada yang hadir bahwa Sa'd adalah pamannya dan meminta jika ada yang memiliki paman yang lebih baik, agar memperkenalkannya.

Setelah berjanji setia kepada Islam di masa muda belia dan menerima pendidikan serta pelatihan di Dar Al-Arqam, beliau memiliki keistimewaan sebagai salah satu sahabat terkemuka yang pertama kali memeluk Islam. Di antara mereka yang terpengaruh oleh ajaran Abu Bakr Siddiq adalah 'Uthman bin 'Affan, Zubair bin 'Awam, 'Abdur-Rahman bin 'Auf, Talhah bin 'Abdullah, dan Sa'd bin Abi Waqas. Para sahabat pertama ini, untuk menghindari penganiayaan kaum Quraisy, biasa bersembunyi di daerah-daerah sepi di sekitar Makkah untuk salah. Pada suatu kesempatan, beliau berada di tempat persembunyian rahasia bersama beberapa sahabatnya, tenggelam dalam meditasi dan salah, ketika beberapa orang jahil dan nakal menemukan mereka. Mereka mulai mengejek dan mencemooh mereka. Sa'd bin Abi Waqas mengambil tulang unta yang tergeletak di dekatnya dan melemparkannya dengan kuat seperti anak panah. Tulang itu mengenai dahi salah seorang pemuda dan darah menyembur keluar. Mereka semua panik dan melarikan diri, saling bertabrakan satu sama lain. Inilah langkah pemberani pertama yang diambil Sa'd bin Abi Waqas dalam membela Islam untuk mengecilkan nyali musuh-musuhnya, dan itu adalah langkah yang sangat efektif.

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas adalah seorang yang memiliki banyak sifat mulia, tetapi ada dua sifat khusus dalam karakternya yang menjadikan

yang membuatnya menonjol di antara yang lain. Yang pertama adalah ketika Nabi ﷺ melihat kemahirannya memanah dalam perang Uhud, beliau sangat terkesan dan sangat senang sehingga beliau berharap agar ia terus melanjutkan pekerjaannya yang baik dan berkata bahwa bahkan kedua orang tuanya bisa dikorbankan untuknya. Ini tentu merupakan kehormatan besar yang membedakannya dari yang lain. Kualitas baik lainnya adalah bahwa ia diberkahi kemampuan untuk berdoa kepada Allah ﷻ dan doanya dikabulkan. Nabi ﷺ telah berdoa kepada Allah ﷻ agar Dia mengabulkan permohonan Sa'd bin Abi Waqas رضي الله عنه. Setelah itu, setiap kali Sa'd bin Abi Waqas berdoa kepada Allah Yang Mahakuasa, doa dan permohonannya dikabulkan. Semua sahabat lainnya sangat menghargainya.
content_ar: عندما Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah # read the letter قال sorrowfully that he knew what was the urgent and المهم work that ‘عمر Farooq 8 had for me. He wanted to keep alive a man who was not born to live in this الدنيا forever. Then كتب a letter to the Caliph ‘عمر Farooq -%. بعد greeting him قال he knew what work he had for me. He was in the مسلم الجيش and at that time the المسلمون were involved in severe problems; he could not leave them alone at that البالغ الأهمية phase, nor could he separate himself from them, إلا إذا it was الله Almighty’s Will to decide about their fate and his. He appealed to the Caliph to consider him helpless للطاعة his commands in this matter and requested him to give him permission to stay with the المجاهدين of الإسلام. عندما ‘عمر Farooq # received this letter and read it his eyes brimmed with tears. The الصحابة sitting around him were surprised to see this and asked him إذا here was some tragic news, and إذا the القائد of the مسلم forces, Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 4& had died. رد: “No, but الموت is very near him now.” And his guess turned out to be correct. A few days later Abu ‘Ubaidah bin Al-Jarrah 3 was struck by the deadly plague. In his last days he made the following dying statement to his armed forces: “Today I am giving you some advice; إذا you accept you it will find peace and security to be your الصحابة: Establish Salét, be regular in your الصلوات. Fast خلال the month of Ramadan. Give sacrifices and be charitable often, Encourage and instruct بعضهم بعضاً to do الخير actions. Wish your rulers well and be loyal to them. Never deceive your rulers. Be careful that you perform your duties and are not lost in worldly pursuits. @ Listen carefully to what I have to say next. Even إذا a man gets to live to be a thousand years old, يوماً ما he will be in the same position in which you see me today. No one can escape الموت. My greetings of peace to all of you; may there be countless البركات of الله the Merciful on you all.” Then he turned to Mu’adh bin Jabl 8 and said: “Lead the men well in الصلوات. الله Hafiz.” And with these words on his lips he passed away. “To الله we belong and to Him we return.” Mu’adh bin Jabl 4 stood up and told the people with quivering lips and a trembling voice: “Brothers in الإسلام! You are sunk in الحزن today for a man who was a more الخير hearted than any of us. He had a pure القلب free from malice or الحسد; and he was a man who loved الآخرة and was the well-wisher of each one of us. He was a man who was given the title of ‘آمين-ul-الأمة’ and خلال his very lifetime on this earth he was given the glad tidings that he would go to الجنة by no less a person than النبي ﷺ 3 himself. He was a very lucky man indeed! Let us all pray that his position in الجنة ever exalted. May الله 3% shower his البركات on Him.” Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas Messenger of الله # said: “Sa‘d! shoot your arrows, May my الأم and الأب be sacrificed for you.” Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas A tall tanned sturdily built youth with broad shoulders and curly hair he was a picture of devotion to الله Almighty. He led a austere and pure الحياة, kept awake at nights in worship, and was courageous and yet forbearing. A God-fearing man he was generous, humble and selfsacrificing. This fortunate Companion had received the glad tidings that he would go to الجنة, from the lips of النبي ﷺ 38 himself. He had أيضاً prayed to الله Almighty that the supplications of Sa’d bin Abi Wagas should be answered and that his arrow should always find its mark. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas had been a member of the group of Companion who had pledged [loyalty to الله 3 and His النبي 3 at what came to be known as_ the Bai‘at-Rizwan. These الصحابة are mentioned in the Noble القرآن الكريم, and Sa‘d bin Abi Wagas 3% was one of them. He was one of those brave soldiers who took part in the المعركة of Uhud and were known for their fearlessness. In this المعركة this experienced archer had rained his arrows on the العدو and النبي ﷺ 3% had been لذا pleased with him that قال to him: “Sa'd shoot your arrows; May my الأم and الأب be Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas .# became a مسلم at the age of seventeen. He was educated and trained in the Dar Argam' and was with النبي ﷺ 38 ' Argaam was the name of a Companion. The newly converted المسلمون used o meet secretly at his house to study the teachings of الإسلام. They were forced to meet in secret لأن of their persecution by the Quraish. (Translator’s note} at the Shi‘ab Abi Talib,' bearing with الصبر and fortitude the difficulties and الشدة of the siege. The whole of his الحياة was spent displaying his prowess and achieving amazing exploits in the المعركةfield. Every moment of his الحياة deserves study by the المسلمون of today and ولا سيما the youth in the military. In the famous place called Jasr in Iraq he opposed the Iraqi الجيش. They came on to the المعركةfield looking very warlike and arrogantly clapping their breasts. There was very heavy fighting and the attack of the العدو was لذا very forceful and severe that four thousand المجاهدين were الشهيدed ina single day. The Caliph, ‘عمر Farooq - was very sad to hear this tragic news, and decided to go to Iraq and lead the مسلم الجيش himself. He ordered that ‘علي # be putin charge of affairs at the capital, Al-المدينة المنورة, and with some المجاهدين reinforcements set out for Iraq. عندما ‘Abdur-Rahman_ bin ‘Auf 4 came لمعرفة that ‘عمر Farooq was on his الطريق to Iraq, he was very upset and set out to stop him. He caught up with him just a short distance from Al-المدينة المنورة and persuaded him not to leave the capital, as the situation was البالغ الأهمية. He tried to convince him that his presence was الجوهري in the capital, as the المسلمون were facing a difficult and delicate situation. The مسلم Ummali needed his services very badly, and there were plenty of experienced strategists, and courageous lions, who could be sent to crush the kuffar. He suggested it would be better to entrust the military leadership to an experienced and fearless General. These arguments convinced ‘عمر Farooq + and he summoned ‘علي 4 and some other prominent Companion to a meeting; they all agreed that ‘عمر Farooq -# should return to the capital, and an experienced القائد be chosen to lead the مسلم forces in Iraq. Giving in to the collective opinion of the الصحابة, ‘عمر Farooq # returned to Al-المدينة المنورة. حين there he called another meeting of the Companion to advise him on the choice of a General. He asked them to suggest he name of a heroic general who * The المسلمون (men, women and الأولاد) were besieged in the house of Abi Talib in مكة المكرمة. It was a very difficult time and the siege lasted three years. Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas -, the great General, was the cousin الأخ of النبي ﷺ's الأم, Aminah. حين عندما he came with some of his الصحابة into the presence of النبي ﷺ 38, he very proudly told those present that he was his uncle and asked إذا anybody had a better uncle, they should introduce him. بعد pledging allegiance to الإسلام in the flower of his youth and receiving his education and training in Dar-ul-Arqam, he had the privilege of being among the أولاً distinguished Companion to accept الإسلام. Among those to be influenced by the teachings of أبو بكر Siddiq > were ‘عثمان bin ‘Affan, Zubair bin ‘Awam, ‘AbdurRahman bin ‘Auf, Talhah bin ‘Abdullah and Sa‘d bin Abi Wags 4 2, -+—. These أولاً Companion, من أجل escape the persecution of the Quraish used to hide in the deserted areas near مكة المكرمة to pray. On one occasion he was in a secret hiding place with some of his الصحابة absorbed in meditation and الصلاة, عندما some mischievous and rowdy elements spotted them. They started to scornfully jeer at them. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas -# picked up the bone of a camel lying nearby and threw it forcefully like an arrow. It hit one of the boys on his forehead and blood spurted out. Panic-stricken they all fled, stumbling and tumbling against بعضهم بعضاً. This was the أولاً courageous step taken by Sa‘d bin Abi Waqs - in support of الإسلام to cow down its enemies, and it was a very effective step. Sa’d bin Abi Waqas # was a man who possessed many great qualities, but there are two special traits of his الأخلاق which make him stand out among the others. The أولاً one is that عندما النبي ﷺ 3 saw his marksmanship in the المعركة of Uhud, he was لذا impressed and لذا happy that he wished him to continue his الخير work and said even his الوالدان could be sacrificed for him. It is certainly a great honour which distinguishes him from others. Another الخير quality was that he was blessed with the ability to pray to الله 38 and have his supplications answered. النبي ﷺ 3% had prayed to الله 3% that He should grant acceptance to the entreaties of Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 3. بعد this كلما Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas prayed to الله Almighty, his الصلوات and supplications would be answered. All other Companion used to value him highly.
